overall_assessment: The corrected baseline closes the earlier fail-open and subject-scoped abuse-control gaps on the bounded public auth routes, but it is still not a clean security close: reset failures remain timing-asymmetric and the canonical reset password rule is still minimum-only.
recommended_path: Keep RR-AUTH-04 open until the invalid-reset path is equalized or explicitly accepted as residual debt with reopen triggers. Separately classify the missing common/breached-password screening as bounded follow-up debt if the RR-AUTH-04 scope remains frozen.

findings.title: Invalid reset attempts still leak account state through timing
findings.rationale: Both tenant and landlord reset flows still diverge sharply on the invalid path. When the email is missing, the services call absorbMissingResetWorkFactor(), which performs extra hash work before returning an invalid-token error. When the email exists but the token is wrong, the services go straight to consumeForUser(), which only performs the lookup-hash delete and throws. That leaves a measurable timing gap between 'no such account' and 'real account, wrong token' on public reset endpoints even though the response payload is normalized.
findings.suggested_action: Equalize the invalid-reset work on the existing-account path with the missing-account path, or add a shared timing-normalization boundary around all invalid reset exits. Add bounded coverage that proves missing-email and wrong-token failures stay in the same timing bucket.

findings.title: Canonical reset password policy still stops at length and confirmation
findings.rationale: ResetPasswordRequestContract::canonicalPasswordRules() only enforces required|string|min:8|max:32|confirmed. The refreshed tenant and landlord tests prove only that sub-minimum passwords are rejected without consuming the token; they do not add any common-password or breached-password screening. That leaves the remaining password-enabled surfaces able to accept well-known weak passwords that satisfy the minimum length.
findings.suggested_action: Either extend the canonical reset-password rule with a common/breached-password control or record this explicitly as non-blocking residual debt outside RR-AUTH-04 closure logic.
